articles.title: MDMK general secretary Vaiko loses cool over DMK alliance question; supporters try to attack reporters
articles.description: MDMK leader Vaiko lost his composure at a press meet when questioned about his criticism of the DMK. He alleged widespread corruption during the DMK's tenure, particularly in land registration. When pressed on why he hadn't raised these issues earlier, Vaiko cited coalition ethics. The situation escalated with his supporters attempting to confront reporters, but police intervention diffused the tension.

articles.title: Huge fire in naphtha pipeline in Bengal's Haldia, many injured: watch what happened| East Midnapore
articles.description: At least 20 people were injured, including six critically, after a major fire broke out in a naphtha pipeline at Haldia Petrochemicals Limited in East Midnapore district, West Bengal, early on June 30. The blaze erupted around 5 a.m., prompting the deployment of nearly 10 fire tenders to control the flames. Several shanties located near the pipeline were gutted in the fire. According to Haldia Petrochemicals Limited, preliminary findings suggest the incident may have occurred near an unauthorized naphtha theft point. The company said naphtha is a highly combustible and extremely flammable hydrocarbon and reiterated its warnings against illegal access to petroleum products. Authorities have launched an investigation to determine the exact cause of the fire.

articles.title: From OTT revival to franchise success: ‘Bharatanatyam 3: Rasaleela’ set for 2028 release
articles.description: Saiju Kurup’s Bharatanatyam franchise expands with the announcement of the third film Rasaleela after the success of Mohiniyattam, continuing the popular Malayalam film series.

articles.title: Extortion syndicates based abroad behind firing incidents in Maharashtra: CM Fadnavis
articles.description: Organized extortion syndicates operating from abroad are orchestrating firing incidents in Maharashtra, Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis revealed. These groups use VPNs and virtual numbers to threaten individuals and hire local criminals for attacks when demands are not met. The government is working with the Centre to apprehend those involved and dismantle these criminal networks, including those linked to gangster Lawrence Bishnoi.

articles.title: Why Indian women abroad are investing more from the West than the Gulf
articles.description: Indian women living overseas are participating in cross-border investing in record numbers, with female investor participation rising nearly 70% over the past year. However, the trend is far more pronounced in Western countries than in the Gulf, highlighting how migration patterns and financial independence shape investment behaviour.

articles.title: 'If you spend all day in a fish market, you’ll carry the smell of fish': Harsh Goenka shares an important life lesson that can change how you see opportunities
articles.description: Industrialist Harsh Goenka shared a powerful social media message highlighting how our daily environments profoundly shape our perspectives. He illustrated this by comparing exposure to a fish market versus a perfume shop, and the company of complainers versus grateful individuals. Goenka's insight emphasizes that our surroundings can either foster a focus on problems or cultivate an outlook of endless possibilities, urging a conscious choice in our daily associations.

articles.description: 'Travel with Karen's' groups are made up of people who enjoy meeting local influencers and intellectuals, historical and cultural experts, and come away “ignited and delighted”.

articles.title: ‘You were our lost... ’: Supriya Pathak’s words for Mira Kapoor throw light on the kind of relationship she shares with her daughter-in-law: 3 takeaways for women
articles.description: Veteran actress Supriya Pathak shares a heartwarming bond with her daughter-in-law, Mira Kapoor, emphasizing genuine acceptance over traditional roles. Pathak views Mira not as a daughter-in-law, but as a beloved daughter who has always belonged. This relationship, built on emotional safety and mutual respect, offers a refreshing contrast to common in-law dynamics, highlighting how warmth and openness foster deep family connections.

articles.title: Novak Djokovic's wife Jelena Djokovic shares special life update while Serbian star escapes major Wimbledon upset against Wu Yibing
articles.description: Novak Djokovic battled past Wu Yibing in a tough four-set opener at Wimbledon, admitting he wasn't at his freshest after a long break since the French Open. Meanwhile, his wife Jelena marked her 40th birthday with a heartfelt social media post, thanking fans for their wishes despite celebrating a little late. The couple remains active through the Novak Djokovic Foundation, which supports early childhood education across Serbia, even as Novak chases a record 25th Grand Slam title this summer.

articles.title: Sarla's Relatives Say Her Parents Are 'Like Living Corpses' Amid Ongoing Tragedy | News18
articles.description: An overwhelming sense of grief continues to surround Sarla's family as her relatives describe the emotional condition of her parents in the aftermath of the tragedy. Speaking to the media, relatives claimed that Sarla's parents are "like living corpses," saying the loss has left them emotionally shattered and struggling to cope with daily life. The family's remarks underscore the profound emotional impact of the incident as they continue to seek justice and await further developments in the investigation. In this report, we bring you the family's emotional account, the latest updates in the case, and the ongoing calls for accountability n18oc_breaking-news

articles.title: Madhavan could not believe his eyes after watching a 3-year-old drummer leading an orchestra
articles.description: A three-year-old Russian drumming prodigy, Lyonya Shilovsky, has captivated the internet after actor Madhavan shared a video of his astonishing performance. Lyonya, who began drumming at one, confidently led a full orchestra, showcasing remarkable musicality and stage presence without sheet music. His talent, first recognized on a Russian talent show, continues to amaze viewers worldwide, proving exceptional abilities can emerge at a very young age.

articles.title: West Bengal Removes 77 Muslim Communities From OBC List As Assembly Passes Two Key Amendment Bills
articles.description: The West Bengal Assembly has enacted two amendment bills that eliminate 77 Muslim communities from the OBC list, aligning with a Calcutta High Court ruling.

articles.title: Warren Buffett skips donation to Gates Foundation amid Epstein review: Report
articles.description: Warren Buffett, the chairman of Berkshire Hathaway, has reportedly postponed his customary mid-year donation to the Gates Foundation. This delay stems from his desire to await the outcomes of a review concerning the foundation's connections to the late Jeffrey Epstein. Buffett is expected to make his decision later this year, potentially around his Thanksgiving letter, according to sources cited by The Wall Street Journal.

articles.title: Zulu Proverb of the Day: 'Imvu ibulala indhlovu' - 'A sheep kills an elephant'
articles.description: Zulu Proverb of the Day: A Zulu proverb, "Imvu ibulala indhlovu," meaning "A sheep kills an elephant," powerfully illustrates that strength isn't solely about size. It highlights how persistence, strategy, and unity can overcome seemingly insurmountable odds. This ancient wisdom remains remarkably relevant today, reminding us that even the smallest efforts can create significant change and that no one should be underestimated. It champions resilience and humility in a world often focused on dominance.

articles.title: Former Titans player Chris Johnson's resurfaced video is hard to watch after his life-changing announcement
articles.description: Former NFL and Tennessee Titans star player Chris Johnson's recent ALS diagnosis has cast a poignant new light on a video of him coaching at the 2025 Senior Bowl. Despite battling the disease, which has affected his speech, Chris Johnson displayed remarkable strength and a commitment to mentoring young talent.

articles.title: 'Love To Have Him Back': Sergio Gor Confirms PM Modi's US Invitation, Hints At Trump India Visit Next Year
articles.description: US Ambassador Sergio Gor confirmed that PM Modi has been invited to visit the US again. He also hinted that Donald Trump could visit India next year.

articles.title: 'RUN ENDS IN SOMALIA': FBI Captures $250M Pandemic Fraud Kingpin After Years On Run
articles.description: A major breakthrough has emerged in one of the biggest pandemic fraud investigations in US history. The US Department of Justice says alleged fraud leader Abdikerm Eidleh has been arrested in Mogadishu, Somalia, after spending more than four years on the run. Federal authorities accuse him of playing a key role in a massive $250 million COVID-era meal reimbursement scam, allegedly collecting millions in illegal payments while submitting fraudulent claims through the Minnesota Department of Education. Investigators say the operation diverted funds meant to feed children during the pandemic. Eidleh now faces 31 criminal charges, including wire fraud, bribery and money laundering, as prosecutors continue pursuing accountability in the landmark case.

articles.title: SC Justice Coney Barrett Jolts Trump Camp; MAGA Erupts Over Supreme Court Election Decision | WATCH
articles.description: A U.S. Supreme Court ruling on mail-in ballots has triggered strong reactions from several Republican lawmakers and conservative commentators. Justice Amy Coney Barrett, nominated by President Donald Trump during his first term, joined Chief Justice John Roberts and the court's liberal justices in upholding state laws that allow certain mail-in ballots postmarked by Election Day to be counted after Election Day. The decision drew criticism from figures including Senator Eric Schmitt and other prominent conservatives, who argued it could affect election integrity. Others noted that late-arriving ballots account for only a small share of total votes and that existing verification procedures remain in place.

articles.title: H-1B Visa Holders Say Texas Is Turning Them Away — Here's Why
articles.description: H-1B visa holders in Texas are facing challenges with driver's license renewals due to expired visa stamps, despite being lawfully present as confirmed by immigration attorneys.

articles.title: US Stock Market: Economic resilience collides with expensive valuations on Wall Street
articles.description: The US economy shows surprising strength with robust job growth and consumer spending, yet Wall Street is faltering. Investors are wary of lofty valuations, especially in the AI sector, as tech giants increasingly rely on debt for expansion. While chipmakers surge, the 'Magnificent Seven' face headwinds, creating market volatility despite positive economic fundamentals.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Another shocker as Netherlands knocked out by Morocco after shootout drama
articles.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32: Morocco defeated the Netherlands in a penalty shoot-out to advance to the last 16 of the World Cup on Monday after a thrilling battle in Monterrey finished 1-1 after extra-time. An enthralling match had gone to extra time after Issa Diop had glanced in a dramatic equaliser for Morocco in the first minute of stoppage time as the Netherlands closed in on victory.

articles.title: Germany horror, 1st visuals: Gunman opens fire at youth shelter for women & children in Stade
articles.description: Germany is in shock after a deadly shooting at a youth welfare facility in Stade left six people dead, including four women. Police launched a major operation, arrested two people after a dramatic vehicle stop, and sealed off the area as forensic teams searched the scene. Authorities have ruled out a terrorist motive for now and are investigating whether the attack was driven by personal circumstances. Watch this report for eyewitness accounts, police updates, and the latest developments from one of Germany's deadliest shootings this year.
